build.buildCommand:
python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/forge-ci.yml'))" && echo "yaml OK" && git add .github/workflows/forge-ci.yml && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(ci): swap cli npm run build → npm run bundle in harness job

The `harness` job in forge-ci.yml ran `cli npm run build` (tsc only),
but several harnesses assert that `cli/assets/.forge/...` mirrors the
repo-root templates. `cli/assets/` is gitignored (cli/.gitignore:3)
and only materialised by `cli/scripts/bundle-assets.mjs` (invoked via
`npm run bundle` = `build` + bundle-assets, and chained from
`prepack`). Without the bundle, the mirror doesn't exist on CI
runners and the assertion fails by construction.

Concretely : `t5-otel-dartastic.test.sh::_test_t53_l1_011_cli_assets_mirror`
(FR-T53-C-003) was failing in CI with :
  [FR-T53-C-003] cli/assets pubspec.yaml.tmpl missing:
    /home/runner/work/forge/forge/cli/assets/.forge/templates/archetypes/mobile-only/pubspec.yaml.tmpl
The file is present locally because bundle-assets.mjs runs as part of
`npm run bundle` (or `npm pack`), but the harness job never invoked
that script.

Fix : swap `npm run build` for `npm run bundle` in the harness job's
setup step. Build is a strict subset of bundle so there's no
duplication, and the asset mirror becomes available to every
downstream harness step.

Local verification :
- `rm -rf cli/assets && cd cli && npm run bundle` →
  `bundle-assets: 1166 files → assets/`
- `bash .forge/scripts/tests/t5-otel-dartastic.test.sh --level 1` :
  13/13 GREEN (was 12/13 — _test_t53_l1_011 was RED).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3 && git push 2>&1 | tail -3
build.testCommand:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(ci): T5.1.B vitest skip + t5 harness contract alignment

Two follow-up CI fixes on top of f0089c5 (post-T5.3) :

- `cli/test/e2e/archetypes-smoke.test.ts` — `ctx.skip()` was failing
  with `Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'skip')` on CI :
  vitest 2.1.x's `it.each(rows)(name, cb)` does NOT pass a TestContext
  to the parameterised callback, so a runtime skip is not available.
  Refactored to partition archetypes at module-load and use
  `it.skip.each(...)` for the toolchain-missing set. Result on CI (no
  flutter/cargo/buf on PATH) : 1 passed + 2 skipped, with the skipped
  declarations visible in the report (`scaffolds <name> [missing on
  PATH: ...] (skipped — ADR-T51-001)`) instead of a silent return.

- `.forge/scripts/tests/t5.test.sh` — four assertions were stale vs
  the templates evolved in T5.1.E (`t5-cargo-pin-refresh` +
  `t5-bin-server-deps`, 2026-05-16) and never updated alongside :
    * `_test_t5_006` : `connectrpc` is now in inline-table form
      ({ version = "=0.3.3", features = ["axum"] }) to enable the
      axum feature required by `Router::into_axum_router()` ;
      `buffa` / `buffa-types` corrected from invalid `=0.3.3` (never
      published) to `=0.3.0` per ADR-T5CPR-001 / REVIEW.md 2026-05-16.
      Regex extended to accept both encodings + updated pin.
    * `_test_t5_009` : transport.yaml bumped 1.1.0 → 1.2.0 in commit
      60fa53b (REVIEW.md ledger 2026-05-16). Assertion follows.
    * `_test_t5_013` : `into_axum_service()` → `into_axum_router()`
      (axum 0.8 stricter `Service` bounds, T5.1.E doc inside the
      template). Pattern accepts both names for adopter compatibility.
    * `_test_t5_015` : the OTel-layer-outside-connectrpc post-condition
      moved from `transport_connect.rs.tmpl` to the call site
      (`bin-server/main.rs.tmpl`) because axum 0.8 rejected the
      generic `L: Layer<...>` adapter signature (T5.1.E
      t5-bin-server-deps). The test now asserts (1) no executable
      `.layer(` inside the adapter AND (2) a `.layer(` AFTER
      `transport_connect::into_router` in bin-server/main.rs.tmpl.
      FR-T5-CC-013 post-condition unchanged.

The 4 t5 harness failures are exactly the "ratification grep-only sans
build" pattern flagged in user memory `t5_connect_codegen_lesson.md` :
templates evolved to fix Rust compilation errors caught by
`flutter analyze`-equivalent (L2 cargo check), but the L1 grep
assertions were never refreshed. This commit closes the contract gap.

Local verification :
- `bash .forge/scripts/tests/t5.test.sh --level 1` : 25/25 GREEN
  (was 21/25 — 4 stale assertions).
- `npx vitest run` with normal PATH : 72/72 GREEN.
- `npx vitest run` with simulated empty Flutter/Cargo/buf PATH :
  1 passed + 2 skipped (3) — skip declarations render correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3 && git push 2>&1 | tail -3
